people.name: Tim Bruised Head
people.biography: Tim Bruised Head is an Indigenous Canadian actor and stunt performer for film and television productions.

people.name: Jordan Dodds
people.biography: Jordan Dodds is a Canadian rodeo rider, horse trainer and wrangler who works as stunt performer, stunt double and stunt coordinator for film and television productions. Together with his wife Robyn he owns Dodds Ranch and organizes the annual Top Gun Horse Sale in Ponoka, Alberta, Canada.

people.name: Lindsay Woolsey
people.biography: Canadian Lindsay Woolsey works as stunt performer and animal wrangler for film and television productions.

people.name: Martin Cochingco
people.biography: Canadian Martin Cochingco works as stunt performer for film and television productions.

people.name: CJ Jones
people.biography: CJ Jones (born September 29, 1950) is a deaf American actor residing in Los Angeles. He is one of the subjects of See What I'm Saying: The Deaf Entertainers Documentary. Jones made his feature film debut with Edgar Wright's Baby Driver, in which he portrays Joseph, the deaf foster father of Ansel Elgort's protagonist

people.name: Dusty Bews
people.biography: Dusty Bews is an actor and stuntman.

people.name: Dustin Brooks
people.biography: Dustin Brooks is known for his work on War for the Planet of the Apes (2017), The Incredible Hulk (2008) and Watchmen (2009).

people.name: Jennifer Clarke
people.biography:
Jennifer was born in Vancouver BC, Canada where she started recreational gymnastics at the early age of 5. Jennifer received a Full-ride NCAA DIV-1 gymnastics scholarship to Towson University in Baltimore, MD where she completed her Bachelors of Science degree, in Business. Post University, Jennifer's business career thrived, traveling globally and receiving consecutive promotions into higher levels of executive management.

Realizing that performing, training, and acrobatics provided a sense of fulfillment that could not be replaced, Jennifer jumped wholeheartedly into the world of stunt performing and hasn't looked back. Her gymnastics background has given her a foundation that has helped her truly excel as a stunt woman.

people.name: Adrian Young
people.biography: Canadian Adrian Young works as flying director, (aerial) stunt coordinator and stunt performer, human load rigger, and actor for stage, film and television productions.

people.name: Leonard Provost
people.biography: Leonard Provost is a Canadian actor and stunt performer.

people.name: Kelsi Chartrand
people.biography: Kelsi Chartrand was born on 17 December 1990 in Mission, British Columbia, Canada. She is a stunt person and actress, known for X2: X-Men United (2003), The Last of Us (2023) and Little Fish (2020).

people.name: Afton Rentz
people.biography: Canadian Afton Rentz works as an actress, stunt performer and fight director for stage, film and television productions. She's the co-creator, fight director and star of the web series "Mental Case".

people.name: Christian Fraser
people.biography: Christian Fraser is a film and television actor and works as stunt performer and stunt double. He was part of the 35-men stunt team involved in the initial battle scene of Alejandro González Iñárritu's feature The Revenant, filmed in the Bow River west of Cochrane, Alberta, Canada.

people.name: Marny Eng
people.biography: Marny Eng (sometimes credited as Marney Eng, Marnie Eng or Marni Eng) is a Canadian stuntwoman and stunt coordinator who has performed stunts in well over 100 films since the early 1990s. She has been the stunt double for numerous famous actresses such as Lucy Liu, Christina Ricci, Kristin Kreuk and Rachael Leigh Cook. As an actress, she is most famous for portraying Tabitha, the Samara Morgan parody from Scary Movie 3.

people.name: Vincent-Olivier Noiseux
people.biography: Vincent-Olivier Noiseux is a professional dancer from Quebec, Canada. He is best known for being on the first season of So You Think You Can Dance Canada. He has danced with multiple world-reknowed artists including Katy Perry, Alicia Keys and Jennifer Lopez.

people.name: Sally Bishop
people.biography: Sally Bishop is a Canadian rodeo trick rider and works as stunt performer and stunt double for film and television productions.

people.name: Fraser Corbett
people.biography: Fraser Dean Corbett is a stunt artist and an actor. He is known for his work on Godzilla (2014), Deadpool (2016) and Deadpool 2 (2018)

people.name: Chris Webb
people.biography: Actor / Stuntman.

people.name: Shawna Pliva
people.biography: Shawna Pliva is a Canadian film and television actress who also works as stunt performer and stunt double.

people.name: Andrea Ross
people.biography: Andrea Ross is a Canadian stunt performer and stunt double.

people.name: Louise Hradsky
people.biography: A choreographer known for her strong leadership and collaborative spirit, Louise Hradsky is a creative heavyweight with a refined point of view. Originally from Western Canada, she found her home in the film and television industry 20 years ago, working steadily across the globe as a performer, choreographer, and movement coach since.

people.name: Scott Emerson
people.biography: Scott Emerson works as a stunt performer and wrangler for film and television productions.

people.name: Evelyn Gonda
people.biography: Evelyn Gonda (born January 6, 1997) is a Canadian stunt performer, martial artist, and actress.

people.name: Lindsey Dietz
people.biography: Lindsey Dietz works as stunt performer and stunt double for film and television productions.

people.name: Rod Coulter
people.biography: Canadian Rod Coulter works as an actor and stunt performer for film and television productions.

people.name: Christopher J. Byrne
people.biography: Christopher J. Byrne is a director. A lifelong carpenter, he has also been a proud member of the Directors Guild of Canada for 25 years, the American Federation of Musicians as a composer for 20 years, and the Directors Guild of America for 6 years. Byrne holds an Honours degree in History from York University, where he initially trained to become a high school teacher. He began his film career with the Directors Guild of Canada as a Trainee Assistant Director on Norman Jewison’s The REZ, shot in northern Ontario. Within five years, he rose through the Canadian film industry ranks to become First Assistant Director on the Disney/Alliance Atlantis feature Confessions of an Ugly Stepsister (Luxembourg, 1999).

people.name: Ashley Bell
people.biography: Ashley Bell works as assistant director for film and television productions.

people.name: Peter Hoar
people.biography: Peter Hoar is a double BAFTA-winning, Emmy-nominated British film and television director known for his work on Daredevil, Doctor Who, It's a Sin and The Last of Us.

people.name: Craig Mazin
people.birthPlace: Brooklyn, New York City, New York, USA
people.biography:
Craig Mazin (born 1971) is an American writer, director, and producer. He is best known for creating, writing, and producing the HBO historical disaster drama miniseries Chernobyl (2019) and co-creating, co-writing, and executive producing the HBO post-apocalyptic drama series The Last of Us (2023–present), the latter alongside Neil Druckmann. He has won two Primetime Emmy Awards, including Outstanding Writing for a Limited Series, Movie, or Dramatic Special and Outstanding Limited Series.

Prior to gaining attention and acclaim for his dramatic work, Mazin was primarily known for his work on comedy films such as Scary Movie 3 (2003), Scary Movie 4 (2006), Superhero Movie (2008), The Hangover Part II (2011), The Hangover Part III, and Identity Thief (both 2013).

Description above from the Wikipedia article Craig Mazin, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

people.name: Ali Abbasi
people.biography: Ali Abbasi (Persian: علی عباسی, born 1981) is an Iranian-Swedish-Danish filmmaker. He is known for his films "Shelley" (2016), "Border" (2018), "Holy Spider" (2022), and "The Apprentice" (2024). Abbasi also directed the last two episodes of the first season of the series "The Last of Us"". He has received various accolades, including an Un Certain Regard award, four Robert Awards, and a German Film Award, nominations for seven European Film Awards, a Goya Award, and two Guldbagge Awards.  Abbasi attended Tehran Polytechnic until 2002, when he emigrated to Sweden to study architecture at the KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm. After earning his bachelor of arts in 2007, he enrolled at the National Film School of Denmark, earning a degree in 2011 with the short film "M for Markus".

people.name: Crys Walter
people.biography:
Crys Walter is an Albertan Assistant Director with over 10 years of experience in the local film and tv industry. Since graduating from SAIT Polytechnic's FVP Program in 2015, she has spent much of her time experiencing the best that rural Alberta has to offer through a variety of commercial and feature productions, as well as multiple seasons of CBC’s Heartland, Seven24’s Wynonna Earp, Paramount’s Joe Pickett, and FX’s Fargo.

After being honored to find herself on two different DGC Award-winning Directorial teams, she recently decided to take on the role of AD Caucus Representative for the ADC, where her focus has been on the professional development of Alberta’s young film-workers, as well as her efforts to connect ADs across the country. Her passions include night-gardening (because everything thrives during the summer, and that includes business), horror movies, her fiancé and the menagerie of pets they have at home, and forming deep connections with her communities, both professional and personal.

people.name: Jasmila Žbanić
people.birthPlace: Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Yugoslavia [now Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina]
people.biography: Jasmila Žbanić is a film writer and director from Bosnia and Herzegovina, best known for winning the Golden Bear at the International Filmfestival Berlin in 2006 for her feature film "Grbavica". She's a graduate of the Department for Theater and Film Directing at the Academy of Performing Arts, Sarajevo.

people.name: Liza Johnson
people.biography: Liza Johnson (born December 13, 1970) is an American film director, producer, and writer. Along with her collection of short films, Johnson has directed four feature films, including Return (2011), Hateship, Loveship (2013), and Elvis & Nixon (2016).

people.name: Mark Mylod
people.biography:
Mark Mylod is an English director and executive producer of film and television. He began his career directing comedy shows such as Shooting Stars, The Fast Show, and The Royle Family, for which he received two BAFTA TV Awards.

After transitioning into directing episodes of drama series (including Entourage, The Affair, Game of Thrones, and both the British and American versions of Shameless), Mylod became known for his work as a director and executive producer on the HBO drama series Succession (2018–2023), directing 16 episodes and producing 37. He won the Emmy for Outstanding Directing for a Drama Series once and the Directors Guild of America Award for Outstanding Directing – Drama Series once.

Mylod also directed the films Ali G Indahouse (2002), The Big White (2005), What's Your Number? (2011), and The Menu (2022). All of them received mixed or negative reviews with the exception of The Menu, which was critically acclaimed.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Mark Mylod, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

people.name: Stephen Williams
people.biography:
Stephen Williams is a Canadian film and television director. Williams has directed several modern-day television programs, including working as a regular director on the ABC drama series Lost, where he was also a co-executive producer.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Stephen Williams (director), licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

people.name: Kate Herron
people.biography:
Kate Herron (born 1987/1988) is an English director, writer, and producer. She is known for her female-led comedies. She directed and executive produced the first season of the Disney+ series Loki.

Herron began her career writing and directing short films, such as Frank and Rest Stop. She started her television career in 2017, working with Idris Elba on Five By Five, a five-episode drama from The Idris Takeover for BBC Three. The same year, she was a member of Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe under the Entertainment category. Two years later, she directed four episodes of the Netflix series Sex Education. She also directed one episode of Daybreak, another Netflix series.

In August 2019, it was announced she would be directing and executive producing the first season of the Disney+ series Loki.

In October 2023, it was announced that Herron and Briony Redman would be writing an episode of Doctor Who.

In January 2024, it was announced Herron would be a director on the second season of The Last of Us. In March 2024, it was announced that Herron will direct a film based on The Sims video game franchise and will co-write with Briony Redman.

Herron grew up in southeast London, near Thamesmead. She is bisexual. She attended the University for the Creative Arts in Farnham, where she studied Film Production.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Kate Herron, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

people.name: Stephen Nakamura
people.biography:
Stephen Nakamura, one of the most accomplished feature film colourists in Hollywood, brings a wealth of experience to his work. He has collaborated with esteemed directors such as David Fincher, Ridley Scott, Kathryn Bigelow, Spike Lee, and Steven Spielberg, as well as renowned cinematographers Dariusz Wolski, ASC, Claudio Miranda, ASC, Robert Richardson, ASC, and Janusz Kaminski.

Stephen Nakamura’s feature film credits span a wide range of genres and styles, including major releases such as Nyad (Netflix), Napoleon (Columbia Pictures), Doctor Strange and the Multiverse of Madness (Marvel Studios), and nearly all of Ridley Scott’s feature directing work for over 15 years, including The Martian and the Alien franchise.

Further demonstrating his versatility, Nakamura has received multiple awards for his technical and artistic contributions to significant films, T.V. series, and commercials, including Disney+’s Obi-Wan Kenobi, Hennessy’s X.O.: The Seven Worlds, and most recently, HBO’s The Last of Us, for which he was awarded the 2024 Lumiere Award from the Advanced Imaging Society (AIS) for Best Use of High Dynamic Range in an episodic series. In 2018, Stephen Nakamura was accepted into the Academy of Motion Picture Arts & Sciences.

Before joining Company 3, Nakamura was with Technicolour Digital Services, where he got his start in feature film finishing. He was also a colourist with the Post Group, working on significant commercial, music video, television, and feature film projects. He began his career at California Video Centre.

Based at Company 3 Los Angeles. Available virtually through any Company 3 Virtual Outpost.

Information above via their homepage.

people.name: Victoria Thomas
people.biography: Victoria Thomas is known for Once Upon a Time in Hollywood (2019), The Harder They Fall (2021) and Detroit (2017).

people.name: Asad Qizilbash
people.biography:
Asad Qizilbash is a senior executive at Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE), serving as the Senior Vice President of Product within the Studio Business Group and Head of PlayStation Productions. With over 18 years of experience at SIE, Qizilbash has played a pivotal role in expanding the company's entertainment footprint beyond gaming. In 2019, he was instrumental in establishing PlayStation Productions, a division dedicated to adapting SIE's video game franchises into film and television projects. Under his leadership, PlayStation Productions has brought several major titles to the screen, including the film Uncharted (2022) and the television series The Last of Us (2023–present). The latter has been particularly successful, becoming the most-watched show in the history of HBO Max in both Europe and Latin America.

In his capacity as Head of Product at PlayStation Studios, Qizilbash oversees portfolio and strategy planning for PlayStation's exclusive games lineup. He previously served as Vice President of Games Marketing, where he was responsible for growing PlayStation Studios' gaming brands into global entertainment franchises.

Qizilbash's efforts have been recognised with multiple nominations for the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Drama Series for his role as an executive producer on The Last of Us (2023 and 2025).

people.name: Carter Swan
people.biography:
Carter Swan is a senior producer at PlayStation Productions, a division of Sony Interactive Entertainment dedicated to adapting PlayStation's video game franchises into film and television. Based in Los Angeles, his credits include Uncharted (2022), Gran Turismo (2023) and The Last of Us (2023-present).

His educational background includes studies at the Seattle Film Institute.

The Last of Us series earned multiple Primetime Emmy nominations for Outstanding Drama Series in both 2023 and 2025, with Swan serving as an executive producer in both years. Additionally, the series won a Peabody Award for Entertainment in 2024.

people.name: Mary Vernieu
people.biography: Mary Vernieu is a two-time Emmy-nominated casting director.

people.name: Michael Playfair
people.biography: Canadian Michael Playfair works as a (production) sound mixer and recordist for film and television productions.

people.name: Misty Lee
people.biography: Misty Lee is an American voice actress, comedian, and professional magician.

people.name: Aaron Cowan
people.biography:
Aaron Cowan is a visual effects producer at Wētā FX, with a career spanning over thirty years in film and television. He began his journey in visual effects working as a VFX coordinator on The Lord of the Rings trilogy. Over time, he took on roles across production, including studio-side VFX production management on films such as The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe and The Waterhorse. Before joining Wētā FX in 2009, he had also worked client-side and on-set in production roles.

At Wētā FX, Cowan started as a sequence manager on Avatar (2009) and advanced through positions such as production manager on The Adventures of Tintin (2011) and digital producer on Dawn of the Planet of the Apes (2014). As a VFX producer, he has overseen major projects including The Last of Us (2023) and Transformers: Rise of the Beasts (2023), as well as Moon Knight (2022). His work on The Last of Us involved creatures, environments, and set extensions across the season, contributing to Wētā’s visual effects output in that television series. In Transformers: Rise of the Beasts, Cowan was credited as VFX producer, working on the creation of hundreds of shots involving Transformers and the Maximals’ home world.

Cowan’s commitments include fostering efficient collaboration between VFX vendors and production teams, aiming for creative and logistical alignment in high-complexity projects. His contributions have earned him nominations from the Hollywood Professional Association. In 2023, he was nominated for the HPA Award for Outstanding Visual Effects – Theatrical Feature for Transformers: Rise of the Beasts, shared with Matt Aitken, Mike Perry, Kevin Estey, and Jong Jin Choi. In the same year, he was also nominated for Outstanding Visual Effects – Episode or Series Season for The Last of Us (Season One), sharing the credit with Simon Jung, David Hampton, Dennis Yoo, and Ben Roberts.

people.name: Joel Whist
people.biography:
Joel Whist is a mechanical effects supervisor. Known for his films, including War for the Planet of the Apes, The BFG, Godzilla, and Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice, he was nominated for an Academy Award for Best Visual Effects for War for the Planet of the Apes.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Joel Whist, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

people.name: Stephen James
people.biography:
Stephen James is a Canadian visual effects artist. He won an Academy Award in the category of Best Visual Effects for the film Dune: Part Two.

In addition to his Academy Award nomination, he won a Primetime Emmy Award in the category Outstanding Special Visual Effects for his work on the television program The Last of Us. He also won a BAFTA Award for Best Special Visual Effects. His win was shared with Paul Lambert, Gerd Nefzer and Rhys Salcombe.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Stephen James (visual effects artist), licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.

people.name: Espen Nordahl
people.biography:
Espen Nordahl is a Norwegian visual effects artist. He was nominated for an Academy Award in the category of Best Visual Effects for the film Sinners.

In addition to his Academy Award nomination, he won a Primetime Emmy Award in the category Outstanding Special Visual Effects for his work on the television program The Last of Us. His win was shared with Alex Wang, Sean Nowlan, Joel Whist, Stephen James, Nick Marshall, Simon Jung, Dennis Yoo and Jonathan Mitchell.

Description above from the Wikipedia article Espen Nordahl, licensed under CC-BY-SA, full list of contributors on Wikipedia.
